title: Exploratory Feature Analysis and Relationship to Target
summary: Comprehensive univariate and bivariate analysis of all 15 predictive features with statistical tests, effect sizes, feature importance ranking, correlation analysis, and interaction effects exploration.

reports.description: Comprehensive 400+ line analysis report covering all findings, recommendations, and detailed breakdowns
highlights: Recommendations for feature engineering and modeling

analysis_results.description: Unified feature ranking of all 15 features by effect size (Cohen's d or Cramér's V)
Bivariate analysis of numerical features vs conversion target with statistical tests
Bivariate analysis of categorical features vs conversion target with chi-square tests
Full correlation matrix for numerical features including target variable
Feature group analysis showing average effect size by category

deep_dives.description: Detailed breakdown: Multi-pet discount (strongest predictor, 60.67% vs 14.01%)
Detailed breakdown: Debit card on file (existing customer signal, 24.53% vs 14.21%)
Detailed breakdown: Graph DB feature (16.64% vs 14.94%, p=0.0016)
Detailed breakdown: All 41 states with conversion rates and volumes

visualizations.description: Horizontal bar chart of top 14 features by effect size (excluding timestamp)
insights: Mix of numerical and categorical predictors

visualizations.description: 9-panel violin plot comparing numerical feature distributions by conversion status
4-panel analysis of top categorical features (multi-pet, debit, graph DB, state)
Correlation heatmap for numerical features including target variable
2-panel income analysis: distribution comparison and quartile conversion rates
insights: Q4 converts at 19.9% vs Q1 at 12.3%

visualizations.description: Horizontal bar chart of feature group importance by average effect size
2-panel interaction analysis: Multi-pet × Debit and Income × State
insights: Income effect 2× stronger in IL vs FL

implementation_scripts.description: Part 1: Univariate and bivariate analysis with statistical tests
key_functions: Bivariate analysis with t-tests, chi-square, effect sizes

implementation_scripts.description: Part 2: Feature importance ranking, group analysis, and deep dives
key_functions: Deep dives into top categorical features

top_predictors.interpretation: Strongest predictor - existing customers with multiple pets are 4.3× more likely to convert

top_predictors.impact: Q4 converts at 19.9% vs Q1 at 12.3%
top_predictors.interpretation: Strongest numerical predictor - high-income customers significantly more likely to convert
Existing customer signal - having payment method on file increases conversion 1.7×
Price sensitivity confirmed but modest - lower premiums increase conversion
Substantial geographic variation driven by regional income and culture

interaction_effects.interpretation: Strong positive interaction - both signals together yield very high conversion

interaction_effects.finding: Income effect varies 2× by state (IL strongest, FL weakest)
interaction_effects.interpretation: Income impact is state-dependent - suggests need for regional models

graph_db_validation.interpretation: Graph database feature shows modest but statistically significant predictive value

multicollinearity.recommendation: Keep only BASE_PREMIUM for modeling to avoid multicollinearity

feature_engineering: Breed grouping (group rare breeds, reduce 399 to ~50 categories)
Composite scores (existing customer score, value score, engagement score)
modeling_approach: Handle class imbalance (15% conversion rate) - use SMOTE, class weights, or stratified sampling
Use tree-based models (XGBoost, LightGBM) to handle non-linear relationships and interactions
Consider state-specific or region-specific models given geographic variation
Optimize for average sales per quote ($) not just conversion rate
Use temporal split for validation (last month holdout)

statistical_tests_performed: Chi-square tests of independence (categorical vs binary target)
